question_text: Which statement best explains why a needle can pierce skin more easily than a blunt stick, even if both push with the same force?

options.label: A
options.text: A needle is heavier than a stick and therefore pushes harder

options.label: B
options.text: A needle has a much smaller contact area, so the same force creates much higher pressure

options.label: C
options.text: A needle moves faster through the air so it has more energy

options.label: D
options.text: A stick is made of wood which is harder to push through than skin

correct_answer: B) A needle has a much smaller contact area, so the same force creates much higher pressure
explanation: Since pressure = force / area, a needle's tiny point concentrates the force over a very small area, creating extremely high pressure. A blunt stick spreads the same force over a larger area, resulting in lower pressure that is not enough to pierce skin.
